turns.content: I'm getting this error when trying to load a custom model: bevy::app::AssetError. It says the handle is invalid and I can't get the data out of it. What went wrong with my setup?

That usually means you're trying to access an asset before it's been registered or loaded, which causes the Handle<T> to be invalid. The issue is often that you're creating a custom AssetServer without properly handling the load state in your system setup. Think of it like this: if you try to grab a package from a shipping box before it arrives, you'll get nothing. You need to ensure your asset loader runs and registers the handle with the AssetServer before any query tries to read from it.
Okay so I should be using the built-in loaders instead of my own? That would make this easier?

Not necessarily, but you might want to use them as a reference first. The real problem is that beginners often forget that asset loading happens in a specific order within the app lifecycle. If your custom loader doesn't properly implement the AssetLoadPlugin trait and register its handle with the AssetServer before any systems try to query it, the Handle<T> you're getting back will be invalid. You can see this by checking if your system runs after the Assets plugin has finished its setup phase.
